linear.title: HER Grant Resubmission - recover reviewer comments and rebuild workplan
linear.description:
## Outcome

The History of Equal Rights resubmission path is rebuilt from the prior application, reviewer feedback, and remaining work items.

## Source context

* Amount: about $750,000.
* Deadline: 2026-09-30.
* Known work includes reviewer comments, SHPO materials, 2023 budget, historic photos, PDFs, narratives, forms, and [grants.gov](<http://grants.gov>) submission.

## Next action

Recover the reviewer comments and prior 23-file application package, then map each remaining work item into either done, blocked, or next-action status.

## Blockers

* Reviewer comments not yet recovered.
* Original application package location needs confirmation.
* SHPO letter/status needs confirmation.

## Acceptance criteria

* Reviewer comments and prior application package are linked or summarized.
* Reuse/new-work split is clear.
* Budget, narrative, forms, attachments, and submission tasks are either converted to child issues or explicitly marked unnecessary.
* Timeline works backward from 2026-09-30.

linear.title: Schwemm Family Foundation - define archive or interpreter project and draft application
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star has a concrete Schwemm application concept and enough application detail to decide whether to submit before the 2026 window closes.

## Source context

* Amount: about $8,000.
* Window: 2026-05-01 to 2026-06-30.
* Fit note: archives, interpretive signage, or docent program.

## Next action

Recover/download the current application instructions and choose one focused scope: archive supplies/digitization, interpretive signage, or docent/interpreter support.

## Blockers

* Current application details and required attachments need confirmation.
* Scope owner and budget not chosen.

## Acceptance criteria

* Current guidelines are attached or summarized.
* One project scope is selected with rough budget.
* Application requirements are translated into a submission checklist.
* Submit/no-submit decision is made before 2026-06-15.

linear.title: NEA Grants for Arts Projects - start federal registration path and museum fit research
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star has a clear go/no-go for NEA Grants for Arts Projects and knows whether federal registration work must start immediately.

## Source context

* Amount: $10,000 to $100,000.
* Intent/application date noted: 2026-07-09.
* Known gates: [SAM.gov](<http://SAM.gov>), [Login.gov](<http://Login.gov>), [Grants.gov](<http://Grants.gov>), museum/arts fit.

## Next action

Check [SAM.gov/Login.gov/Grants.gov](<http://SAM.gov/Login.gov/Grants.gov>) status and research the strongest museum or arts-program frame for North Star.

## Blockers

* Federal registration can take time.
* Need a strong arts-program deliverable, not just building preservation.
* Match/cash requirements need confirmation.

## Acceptance criteria

* Federal registration status is documented.
* Program fit and eligible project type are summarized with source links.
* Match/cash requirement is known.
* Submit/no-submit decision target is set before 2026-06-30.

linear.title: Hind Foundation - find discrete restoration scope with deposited funds proof
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star knows whether a Hind Foundation request can be made around a discrete restoration scope with required funds already deposited.

## Source context

* Amount: $10,000 to $50,000.
* Deadline: rolling.
* Notes flagged proof of deposited funds and restrictions.

## Next action

Ask finance/board for candidate projects and whether deposited matching/project funds can be documented.

## Blockers

* Proof of deposited funds may be required.
* Project restrictions and eligible cost categories need confirmation.

## Acceptance criteria

* Current Hind guidelines are summarized.
* Candidate restoration scope and budget are identified or ruled out.
* Deposited-funds proof requirement is answered.
* If viable, draft application checklist is created.

linear.title: Long Foundation - research youth arts or education frame
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star knows whether Long Foundation is worth pursuing through a youth arts, education, or interpretation angle.

## Source context

* Amount: $10,000 to $40,000 first-time.
* Fall cycle opens around 2026-07-27.
* Fit note: youth arts/education, not pure preservation.

## Next action

Research current cycle requirements and identify whether North Star has a credible youth/education deliverable.

## Blockers

* Program fit may be weak if framed as preservation only.
* No current youth program scope is documented in this recovery pass.

## Acceptance criteria

* Current guidelines and cycle dates are linked.
* Youth/education fit is rated strong, possible, or weak.
* If possible, one concrete project concept and partner need are named.
* If weak, issue is marked no-go with rationale.

linear.title: NEH Collections Stewardship - verify qualifying North Star collection
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star knows whether it has a qualifying humanities collection for a future NEH Collections Stewardship application.

## Source context

* Deadline noted: 2026-05-11.
* Fit gate: verify qualifying collection.

## Next action

Ask Haley/archives owner what collections exist, where they live, and whether preservation/access work is needed.

## Blockers

* 2026-05-11 deadline appears passed.
* Qualifying collection is unverified.
* Need collection owner, inventory, and preservation/access need.

## Acceptance criteria

* Current/future NEH cycle status is documented.
* Collection existence and ownership are confirmed or ruled out.
* If viable, eligible project activities and required partners are listed.
* If not viable, no-go rationale is recorded.

linear.title: NEH Media Projects - test Julia Morgan media concept
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star knows whether the Julia Morgan / North Star story can support an NEH Media Projects concept before the noted June 2026 deadline.

## Source context

* Deadline noted: 2026-06-25.
* Fit note: Julia Morgan media angle.

## Next action

Confirm eligible media formats, required humanities framing, and whether a credible media partner or producer exists.

## Blockers

* Media partner/producer not identified.
* Humanities framing and audience plan need research.
* Timeline may be too short for a competitive 2026 submission.

## Acceptance criteria

* Current guidelines and deadline are linked.
* Media concept is stated in one paragraph.
* Required partner, budget, and production plan gaps are listed.
* Submit/no-submit decision is made before 2026-06-10.

linear.title: NTHP Preservation Fund - decide June 1 preservation planning application
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star decides whether to pursue the June 1 NTHP Preservation Fund cycle and knows whether PLF membership or another eligibility step is required.

## Source context

* Amount: $2,500 to $5,000.
* Deadline noted: 2026-06-01.
* Note: PLF membership required to receive funds.

## Next action

Email or call NTHP/Saving Places to confirm eligibility, deadline, and whether a planning/consultant scope fits.

## Blockers

* PLF membership or affiliation requirement needs confirmation.
* Scope must likely be planning/consultant/professional service, not general construction.
* Short timeline to 2026-06-01.

## Acceptance criteria

* Eligibility and membership requirement are confirmed.
* A narrow preservation planning scope is selected or ruled out.
* Required match/attachments are listed.
* Submit/no-submit decision is made by 2026-05-20.

linear.title: NCA Cultural District Mini-Grants - research eligibility and cycle
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star knows whether Nevada County Arts Council / Grass Valley-Nevada City cultural district mini-grants are available and worth pursuing.

## Source context

* Note: NCA/GVNC mini-grants; eligibility and cycle unknown.

## Next action

Find current NCAC/GVNC mini-grant contacts, cycle timing, eligibility, and typical award amount.

## Blockers

* Current cycle unknown.
* Eligibility may depend on cultural district geography, fiscal sponsor relationship, or arts programming.

## Acceptance criteria

* Current program owner/contact is identified.
* Cycle date and application path are known or confirmed inactive.
* Fit is rated strong, possible, or weak.
* If viable, next outreach/application step is named.

linear.title: USDA Community Facilities - verify area eligibility and no-go or continue path
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star gets a clear no-go/continue decision for USDA Community Facilities before investing application labor.

## Research finding

Perplexity research rates fit as uncertain-to-possible. The nonprofit/community-facility/museum frame may fit, but the hard gates are property eligibility, USDA Rural Development review, repayment/commercial-credit requirements, and likely low grant percentage. A loan or loan/grant mix is more realistic than a pure grant.

## Next action

Run the exact North Star property address through the USDA eligibility map, then call California USDA Rural Development with a short fact pattern: 501(c)(3), historic house/museum/community facility, needed safety/accessibility/building-envelope work, and likely project range.

## Blockers

* Exact property eligibility must be verified by USDA map/RD staff.
* Need to know whether North Star can take debt or show repayment capacity.
* Need documentation that credit is unavailable at reasonable commercial rates.
* Grant-only path likely weak.

## Acceptance criteria

* Eligibility-map result is captured.
* California USDA RD response is summarized.
* Eligible cost categories are confirmed for doors, safety/accessibility, building envelope, and any bat-related remediation.
* Board-level debt/grant appetite is named.
* Issue ends with continue, pause, or no-go decision.

linear.title: Bat netting and 12 upstairs doors - pursue safety and in-kind funding routes
linear.description:
## Outcome

North Star has a practical funding and in-kind outreach route for removing second-floor bat netting and installing 12 basic upstairs doors.

## Transcript grounding

The 2026-05-07 developer meeting described construction work to remove bat netting on the second floor and a need for 12 basic upstairs doors. The discussion framed doors as sponsor/newsletter/in-kind friendly and bats as a possible worker-safety or public-health angle, not necessarily a normal posted grant.

## Research finding

Perplexity research suggests a $15,000-$55,000 cash target plus $3,000-$10,000 in-kind target. Highest-priority routes are local/business/civic and small preservation grants: Walmart Spark Good, Home Depot Foundation Community Impact, Lowe's/local store support, NTHP Preservation Fund, Community Foundation of Nevada County, Nevada County Arts Council/CA Arts Council if a cultural/safety frame fits, Rotary/Elks/local civic groups, Habitat ReStore, and local hardware/lumber suppliers. OSHA/Cal OES style routes are possible only with a real worker-safety training or county emergency-management partner.

## Next action

Create a one-page ask packet with photos, itemized door count/specs, bat-netting safety rationale, cost estimate, nonprofit status, and recognition offer. Start with Walmart/Home Depot/Lowe's/local civic asks while preserving NTHP as a possible June 1 planning/safety application.

## Blockers

* Need actual contractor or materials quote.
* Need basic door specs and whether labor is volunteer, contractor, or in-kind.
* Need bat/remediation framing that is accurat...
